title: Creating a network with --disable-dns does not accept --dns
body:
### Issue Description

I'm trying to create a bridge network where podman does NOT serve DNS requests but sets the correct DNS server in `/etc/resolv.conf` in the container.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

### 1. Try to create network with no DNS handling (`dnsmasq`) but with `/etc/resolv.conf` handling:
```
$ podman network create test --driver=bridge --ipam-driver=host-local --disable-dns --subnet=10.0.0.0/24 --gateway=10.0.0.1 --ip-range=10.0.0.10-10.0.0.50 --dns=10.0.0.1 
Error: cannot set NetworkDNSServers if DNS is not enabled for the network: invalid argument
```
That failed unfortunately.


### 2. First create the network and later add the resolver IP:
```
$ podman network create test --driver=bridge --ipam-driver=host-local --disable-dns --subnet=10.0.0.0/24 --gateway=10.0.0.1 --ip-range=10.0.0.10-10.0.0.50
test
$ podman network update test --dns-add=10.0.0.1
test
```
This succeeds, but does not have the intended effect:
```
$ podman run --rm --name sh -it --net test alpine sh
/ # cat /etc/resolv.conf
search host.domain
nameserver 8.8.8.8
nameserver 8.8.4.4
/ # 
```
These are the host's DNS servers, not the DNS server specified for the network (10.0.0.1) even though the network's config file has the correct entries:
```json
     "dns_enabled": false,
     "network_dns_servers": [
          "10.0.0.1"
     ]
```

### 3. Set DNS server (again) on container start:
````
$ podman run --rm --name sh -it --net test --dns 10.0.0.1 alpine sh
/ # cat /etc/resolv.conf
search host.domain
nameserver 10.0.0.1
/ # 
````
This is the expected `/etc/resolv.conf`.

The problem is attempt 2. Podman should have set the default DNS server in `/etc/resolv.conf`. As the man page says: 

> --dns=ip
>
> Set network-scoped DNS resolver/nameserver for containers in this network. If not
> set, the host servers from /etc/resolv.conf is used. It can be overwritten on the
> container level with the podman run/create -- dns option. This option can be specified
> multiple times to set more than one IP.

Which, IMHO, implies that the DNS server set in the network config should become the DNS server in the container.



### Describe the results you received

1. Failed command
2. Wronf `/etc/resolv.conf` contents

### Describe the results you expected

A network without `dnsmasq` but with the configured DNS server(s) set in `/etc/resolv.conf` without having to specify them (again) on container creation.

comments.body:
Right now, `podman network create --dns` is only for setting DNS servers for `aardvark-dns`, so disabling DNS causing it to not function is IMO fully expected. I would consider this an RFE and not a bug as such - an addition to the existing DNS flags to allow setting default DNS servers for all containers in the network.

I imagine it would not be that hard to implement on the Netavark side, given all the fields necessary already exist, this would just be repurposing them.

This is expected behavior, the network level dns servers were only ever intended to be used with aardvark-dns.

Now we could add add this, and I certainly understand that the docs are not clear about it but at least network update will not work with that easily. It does not modify the containers resolv.conf, instead we inject these nameservers into the aardvark-dns config. If the network level dns would work we would have to manually edit in case of update which is always extremely awkward but certainly not impossible.

For now setting --dns on the container should work. I would consider allowing network level nameservers for non aardvark-dns networks a new feature request.

@mheon , looks like we got on this at the same time, but good to know we think the same way.

> I imagine it would not be that hard to implement on the Netavark side, given all the fields necessary already exist, this would just be repurposing them.

I don't think this needs any netavark changes at all. I am somewhat sure it could already work correctly in netavark (if we would inject the network level servers into the container_dns_servers like done with --dns on podman run).

I wonder if we should allow network update for that or not

Ok, I clearly misunderstood the intended behavior. From how I read the man page, I understood that `--dns` is for setting `/etc/resolv.conf` and `--disable-dns` is for the disabling the `dnsmasq` server. I don't see any dependency or relation between the two in the docs. The fact that `podman network create` throws an error was a surprise. Even more because `podman network update` succeeds and updates the network config file `network_dns_servers` with the added dns server.


> I would consider allowing network level nameservers for non aardvark-dns networks a new feature request.

Ack. But for a newcomer such as me, `--dns` is in the same category as `--subnet` and `--gateway`. Hence the aforementioned expectations.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
